title: pteropyrum gypsaceum
intro: Pteropyrum gypsaceum, also known as desert broom, is a shrub native to the arid regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and promoting wound healing, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
